model: BST-1 High Power Pro Tactile Bass Shaker (50W)
priceNote: approx 2026 (~$50-60 USD at Parts Express/Amazon; official listing 50W 4-ohm shaker)
keySpecs: 50 watts RMS power handling, 4 ohms impedance
Frequency response tuned for tactile sub-bass lows (turns any surface into a bass transducer)
High-grade aluminum housing, ~3.75 lb weight
Wireable in series (8 ohm), parallel (2 ohm) or series-parallel (4 ohm)
Pairs with SA70 amp (single) or SPA250 amp (2-4 shakers)
whoFor: Budget sim racers adding physical rumble/road-feel to a cockpit or seat without a full motion rig.
imageNotes: daytonaudio.com official page blocked WebFetch (empty body); direct image pulled from authorized reseller willys-hifi.com Shopify CDN (clean white-background product shot). Alt backup: https://smoketronics.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/06/BST_1_18.jpg

keySpecs: 8 localized haptic motors positioned across the seat/back
USB connection for PC + 3.5mm audio jack for console/audio devices
Plug-and-play PC software with multi-channel profiles (no sound card/amp needed)
Customizable and shareable haptic profiles per game title
Supports users up to 6'9" and 330 lbs
whoFor: Sim racers who want haptic body feedback that drops onto any existing seat with zero cockpit modification.
imageNotes: Direct image from official Next Level Racing CDN (cdn.nlr.sh) main product gallery, .webp.

keySpecs: Haptic transducer with universal clamp mount, 5-200 Hz response, 6 oz piston
Included 90W RMS power amplifier @ 2 ohm with wireless remote
Transducer power handling 75W min to 250W max, 2 ohm nominal impedance
Connects via USB-C and RCA/3.5mm; 9.8' quick-release transducer cable
Amp: 10-300 Hz response, ~3.7 lb
whoFor: Sim racers wanting a single powerful bolt-on bass shaker with a matched amp and one-cable setup.
imageNotes: Direct image from official ButtKicker Shopify CDN. URL contains _300x300 thumbnail suffix - can request larger by editing to _2000x2000 or removing the size token if a bigger asset is needed.

keySpecs: 5-channel Class D amp, 30W x4 + 60W x1 @4ohm (4.1) or 30W x5 (5.0)
Dual TPA3116 amplifier chips
Inputs: 3.5mm AUX, optical, Bluetooth; outputs to passive speakers + subwoofer/shakers
All-aluminum CNC unibody chassis with remote control
24-month manufacturer warranty
whoFor: Sim racers driving multiple bass shakers who want an affordable multi-channel amp instead of one amp per shaker.
imageNotes: Direct image from official Fosi Audio Shopify CDN, 1600px main product shot.

keySpecs: Fixed-back single-piece seat eliminates recliner flex
Five-axis roto-molded construction for rigidity
PU molded foam suede cushions, seat belts included
98 unique mounting configurations with NLR seat bracket
Fits waists up to 42", supports up to 550 lb (250 kg)
whoFor: Sim racers upgrading to a rigid, motorsport-grade fixed bucket seat for a serious cockpit.
imageNotes: Direct image from official Next Level Racing CDN (cdn.nlr.sh) product gallery, .png.

model: TR160 V5 Aluminium Racing Simulator Cockpit
priceNote: approx 2026 (official trakracer.com $657 on sale, reg $730; flagship TR160S variant ~$769)
keySpecs: Heavy 160x40mm aluminium profile chassis, zero measurable flex under 16 Nm direct drive
TR One Wheel Mounting System for adjustable wheel position
Adjustable pedal mount compatible with all major pedal brands
Premium seat slider rails included (seat sold separately)
Motion-ready wide-body, low-to-ground design with anodized finish
whoFor: Serious sim racers running high-end direct-drive wheels who need a rigid, motion-ready 80/20 aluminium chassis.
imageNotes: Direct image from official Trak Racer Shopify CDN, anodized black variant. Product task allowed 'TR160S or Alpine TRX'; TR160 V5 is the current-gen aluminium chassis in that line.

keySpecs: Foldable GT-position cockpit that packs away even with electronics installed
NLR Hubs each withstand 150 kg of force for a rigid racing position
Dedicated shifter mount included (Thrustmaster, Logitech, Moza, Fanatec compatible)
Designed for limited-space setups with quick setup and folding
Wheel/pedal/shifter mounting standard
whoFor: Space-constrained sim racers who need a rigid cockpit that folds flat for storage between sessions.
imageNotes: Direct image from official Next Level Racing CDN (cdn.nlr.sh) product gallery, .png.

keySpecs: Seat-mover motion platform for maximum immersion (moves the seat, not the whole rig)
Two-point tactile feedback for road bumps, texture, gear changes, collisions
Includes NLR Platform Manager software, click-and-play with major racing/flight titles on PC
Compact design fits within an existing cockpit footprint, no extra space required
European-made, engineered with commercial motion engineers
whoFor: Enthusiasts chasing the most realistic immersion who want true seat motion on a compact footprint.
imageNotes: Direct image from official Next Level Racing CDN (cdn.nlr.sh) product gallery, .png.

priceNote: approx 2026 (base H3 chassis historically ~$1,949-2,599; H3 with SFU/xtra-smooth gearing configs list ~$2,999)
keySpecs: 3 DOF: pitch + roll + yaw (rear traction loss)
Motion range 40 degrees, speed 75 cm/s (105 deg/s), torque 28 N/m
Acceleration 0.75G transverse / 0.5G longitudinal, ~1400W peak power
Footprint 100x150 cm (3x5.2 ft), 60 cm height; supports up to 440 lb / 200 kg
Includes SimRacingStudio license; seat/wheel/pedals purchased separately
whoFor: DIY-minded sim racers who want full 3-axis motion at a lower price than premium seat-mover rigs.
imageNotes: Direct image from official DOF Reality WordPress wp-content upload, .png (1024x709). Price varies widely by gearing/damper config; official page WebFetch reported $2,999 for the SFU+xtra-smooth build.

priceNote: approx 2026 (original Nova 7 MSRP $179.99, often ~$149; Gen 2 is $199.99)
keySpecs: Dual wireless: 2.4GHz lossless dongle + simultaneous mixable Bluetooth
Custom neodymium magnetic drivers (Nova Acoustic System)
ClearCast Gen 2 AI retractable mic, up to ~25dB noise reduction
~38 hr battery, USB-C fast charge
Multi-platform: PC, PS5, Switch, VR, mobile
whoFor: Sim racers wanting low-latency wireless audio + clear comms across PC and console without a wired tether.
imageNotes: Direct image from official SteelSeries Contentful CDN (images.ctfassets.net) primary PDP shot. This is the 7P (PlayStation) colorway on the shared Nova 7 page; base black Nova 7 is visually identical.

priceNote: approx 2026 (official Govee US $89.99, MSRP $119.99; frequently on sale ~$54-60)
keySpecs: RGBIC segmented lighting, 16 million colors, 41 light beads
215-degree curved lens for wide, bright spread
Built-in mic music/sync modes + 40-60 scene modes
Smart Dial controller; Wi-Fi, works with Alexa & Google Assistant
Razer Chroma sync; ~16.7 in bars
whoFor: Sim racers adding reactive ambient RGB behind a monitor or cockpit for immersion and setup aesthetics.
imageNotes: Govee US official page is a multi-product collection page (no single clean product image via WebFetch); direct H6047-specific product image pulled from authorized retailer lightsdaddy.com Shopify CDN. Amazon ASIN B0B6CQ2LQ2 is the official listing but its media-amazon image src was not exposed to WebFetch.

keySpecs: 10 USB 3.0 ports, up to 5 Gbps data transfer
Individual power switches with blue LED status per port
3 smart charging ports; up to 900mA per port
Included 60W 12V/5A power adapter
Plug-and-play, no drivers required
whoFor: Sim racers connecting many USB peripherals (wheel, pedals, shifter, button box, shakers) from one powered hub.
imageNotes: Direct image from official Sabrent Shopify CDN (1500px). Note: product task said 'HB-BUP3' but the actual current 10-port powered USB 3.0 hub SKU is HB-BU10.
